heading: Allocation of grants
label: 6371g Allocation of grants
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), (2), was amended by , , , and, as so amended, no longer contains a subsec. (b).Section 6371f of this titlePub. L. 101–440, § 8(b)104 Stat. 1015Oct. 18, 1990
Amendments1984—Subsec. (b). inserted reference to Northern Mariana Islands.Pub. L. 98–454
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), (2), was amended by , , , and, as so amended, no longer contains a subsec. (b).Section 6371f of this titlePub. L. 101–440, § 8(b)104 Stat. 1015Oct. 18, 1990
Amendments1984—Subsec. (b). inserted reference to Northern Mariana Islands.Pub. L. 98–454

children.label: A
children.text: Eighty percent of amounts made available under section 6371f(b) of this title shall be allocated among the States in accordance with a formula to be prescribed, by rule, by the Secretary, taking into account population and climate of each State, and such other factors as the Secretary may deem appropriate.1

children.label: B
children.text: Ten percent of amounts made available under section 6371f(b) of this title shall be allocated among the States in such manner as the Secretary determines by rule after taking into account the availability and cost of fuel or other energy used in, and the amount of fuel or other energy consumed by, schools and hospitals in the States, and such other factors as he deems appropriate.1

The Secretary shall allocate 10 percent of the amounts made available under section 6371f(b) of this title in any year for purposes of making grants as provided under in excess of the 50 percent limitation contained in .1section 6371e(b)(2) of this titlesection 6371e(b)(1) of this title

In the case of any State which received for any fiscal year an amount which exceeded 50 percent of the cost of any energy audit as provided in , the aggregate amount allocated to such State under this subsection for such fiscal year (determined after applying paragraphs (1) and (2)) shall be reduced by an amount equal to such excess. The amount of such reduction shall be reallocated to the States for such fiscal year as provided in this subsection except that for purposes of such reallocation, the State which received such excess shall not be eligible for any portion of such reallocation.section 6371b(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: Restrictions on allocations to States
children.label: b Restrictions on allocations to States
children.text: The total amount allocated to any State under subsection (a) in any year shall not exceed 10 percent of the total amount allocated to all the States in such year under such subsection (a). Except for the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Virgin Islands, not less than 0.5 percent of such total allocation to all States for that year shall be allocated in such year for the total of grants to States and to schools and hospitals in each State which has an approved State plan under this part.

children.heading: Prescription of rules governing allocations among States with regard to energy audits
children.label: c Prescription of rules governing allocations among States with regard to energy audits
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Secretary shall prescribe rules governing the allocation among the States of funds for grants for preliminary energy audits and energy audits. Such rules shall take into account the population and climate of such States and such other factors as he may deem appropriate.November 9, 1978

children.heading: Prescription of rules limiting allocations to States for administrative expenses
children.label: d Prescription of rules limiting allocations to States for administrative expenses
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe rules limiting the amount of funds allocated to a State which may be expended for administrative expenses by such State.

children.heading: Reallocations
children.label: e Reallocations
children.text: Funds allocated for projects in any States for a fiscal year under this section but not obli­gated in such fiscal year shall be available for reallocation under subsection (a) of this section in the subsequent fiscal year.
